model_justification: Hybrid subscription+marketplace model recommended based on competitive evidence: (1) Practice Better, Healthie, Nutrium, NutriAdmin all validate subscription SaaS for nutritionist tools ($15-155/mo); (2) Nourish, Fay, Berry Street validate marketplace model for patient-nutritionist matching; (3) Doctolib (€5.8B valuation) proves hybrid subscription-from-providers + free-marketplace-for-patients works at scale in healthcare. The hybrid captures recurring SaaS revenue from nutritionists (AI diet plans, practice management) plus marketplace commission on patient-sourced sessions, diversifying revenue and aligning incentives.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Positioned at LATAM-adjusted mid-range of competitive benchmark ($15-$155 range). Pain intensity is 'high' (from Problem validation), justifying mid-to-high pricing, but LATAM purchasing power parity requires downward adjustment from US-centric $49 mid-point. $39/mo aligns with Nutrium ($25-49/mo) and below Practice Better ($69/mo Professional tier). Additional marketplace commission of ~15% on patient-sourced sessions supplements subscription revenue.

unit_economics.churn_source: Healthcare SaaS SMB monthly churn benchmark of 7.5% (wearefounders.uk 2026 Benchmarks), adjusted to 5% for nutrition-specific SaaS tools with higher switching costs (meal plan libraries, patient data lock-in). SMB SaaS general range: 3-7% monthly (same source).
unit_economics.margin_source: Blended estimate: SaaS portion (~72% revenue) at 70-80% margin (cloudzero.com SaaS Gross Margin Benchmarks 2025), marketplace portion (~28% revenue) at 20-30% margin (industry standard for two-sided marketplaces). Weighted blend ~60%, reduced to 55% for AI compute costs (diet plan generation, NLP). AI-core SaaS runs ~5 points lower on gross margin per 2025 SaaS benchmarks report.
unit_economics.cac_source: Healthcare SMB SaaS CAC $500-$700 (wearefounders.uk 2026 Benchmarks). Adjusted to $350 for LATAM market: lower labor costs for content marketing team, social media acquisition via nutrition communities (Facebook groups, Instagram), lower CPMs in Spanish-language digital advertising. Primary channel: content marketing + community (lower CAC, longer payback).

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $420 pushes LTV/CAC below 1.5x and payback to 14+ months — model becomes unsustainable; must maintain acquisition efficiency through organic channels.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Churn increase to 6% monthly reduces LTV to $495 and ratio to 1.41x — confirms that retention is the critical lever; must invest heavily in switching costs (data lock-in, AI personalization).

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to $43.20 blended ARPU drops LTV to $475 and ratio to 1.36x with 14.7-month payback — limited room for price competition; must differentiate on AI features and LATAM localization rather than price.

model_precedents.model: hybrid (subscription from doctors + free marketplace for patients)
model_precedents.segment: Healthcare providers (doctors, dentists, specialists) in Europe
model_precedents.evidence: €5.8B valuation, 300,000+ paying medical professionals, €139/mo subscription, raised $815M+ total funding. Proved that provider-side subscription + patient-side free marketplace works at massive scale.

model_precedents.model: marketplace (insurance-based, platform takes cut from reimbursements)
model_precedents.segment: Registered dietitians and patients seeking nutrition care in US
model_precedents.evidence: $115M total funding ($70M Series B), 3,000+ RD network, $1B+ valuation, AI-powered platform. Validates marketplace model for dietitian-patient matching at scale.

model_precedents.segment: Health and wellness practitioners (nutritionists, dietitians, coaches)
model_precedents.evidence: $13M funded, 296 G2 reviews at 4.8/5, acquired That Clean Life for meal planning. Validates subscription model for nutritionist practice management tools at $25-155/mo.

model_precedents.segment: Nutrition and wellness practitioners, white-label for platforms like Nourish
model_precedents.evidence: $40.3M total funding ($23M Series B Oct 2024), API-first platform, HIPAA/SOC2 compliant. Validates SaaS infrastructure play for nutrition marketplace backends.

model_precedents.segment: Registered dietitians and patients (insurance-covered nutrition counseling)
model_precedents.evidence: $75M total funding, $500M valuation, booming from Ozempic patient demand. Validates marketplace model for dietitian matching with insurance integration.

model_precedents.model: hybrid (subscription from doctors + free marketplace for patients)
model_precedents.segment: Healthcare providers and patients in LATAM (Mexico, Colombia, Chile, Argentina, Brazil)
model_precedents.evidence: Acquired by DocPlanner Group, dominant healthcare marketplace in LATAM with 330,000+ professionals in Mexico alone. Validates hybrid subscription+marketplace model specifically in LATAM healthcare.

assumptions: ARPU blended at $54/mo: $39 subscription + $15 avg marketplace commission (assumes ~2 marketplace-sourced sessions/mo at ~$40/session with 15% take rate) — estimate based on Doctolib model analogy

Monthly churn at 5%: adjusted downward from 7.5% healthcare SaaS benchmark due to expected switching costs (patient data, meal plan libraries, AI personalization history) — benchmark source: wearefounders.uk 2026
Gross margin at 55%: blended from SaaS (~75%) and marketplace (~25%) portions, reduced 5 points for AI compute costs — sources: cloudzero.com 2025 SaaS margins, industry marketplace benchmarks
CAC at $350: adjusted downward from $500-700 US healthcare SMB benchmark for LATAM market (lower CPMs, lower labor costs for content team) — benchmark source: wearefounders.uk 2026
Primary acquisition channel: content marketing + social media communities — lower CAC but longer ramp; LATAM nutrition Facebook groups and Instagram as primary channels
Marketplace take rate of 15%: positioned below Berry Street/Fay (estimated 20-30% based on dietitian compensation data) to attract supply side in competitive LATAM market
LATAM purchasing power parity adjustment: ~20% discount from US pricing benchmarks for subscription tier
